title: why podman start exit with 125 in a crontab job
body:
I want to have a container monitor script to auto start container if container was down. I run podman stop to shut down a container in advance. Then I set the job into crontab, the podman start command will failed with an exit code of 125, rather than 0. The crontab job is set under root, and the start command in the script is running normally with exit code of 0 if I directly run "bash test_start.sh" in the terminal. Why only will exit with 125 in a crontab job?

[script]
test_start.sh
#! /bin/bash
podman start test
echo $? >> /tmp/test.txt

[crontab]
* * * * * /bin/bash /app/test_start.sh

OS version: redhat 8.2
podman version: 3.2.3

comments.body:
Do you see anything in the log files?
Can you do this with the --restart option?

The best way to monitor a container is using systemd units, check out the `podman generate systemd` command.
If you get a 125 exit code you should get a error message on stderr. We need this to understand your issue.

[script]
test_start.sh
```
#! /bin/bash
export PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin # add this line
podman start test
echo $? >> /tmp/test.txt
```

Do you see anything reported on stderr in syslog from this podman command?

podman start test 2> /tmp/errors.log

Might show you something as well.

I have a feeling this might be SELinux.

> Do you see anything reported on stderr in syslog from this podman command?
> 
> podman start test 2> /tmp/errors.log
> 
> Might show you something as well.
> 
> I have a feeling this might be SELinux.

sorry for the late reply, the error log is as below:
time="2022-01-14T10:16:01+08:00" level=error msg="error loading cached network config: network \"podman\" not found in CNI cache"
time="2022-01-14T10:16:01+08:00" level=warning msg="falling back to loading from existing plugins on disk"
time="2022-01-14T10:16:01+08:00" level=error msg="Error tearing down partially created network namespace for container ed2127df55ce24a5ba3abe6f77f96b3f003a4a9d602b11f693883ac2d65c8556: error removing pod test_test from CNI network \"podman\": could not initialize iptables protocal 0: exec: \"iptables\": executable file not found in $PATH"
Error: unable to start container "ed2127df55ce24a5ba3abe6f77f96b3f003a4a9d602b11f693883ac2d65c8556": error configuring network namespace for container ed2127df55ce24a5ba3abe6f77f96b3f003a4a9d602b11f693883ac2d65c8556: error adding pod test_test to CNI network "podman": failed to locate iptables: exec: "iptables": executable file not found in $PATH

And actually the solution from @zhangguanzhang works, so it results from the $PATH?

> [script] test_start.sh
> 
> ```
> #! /bin/bash
> export PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin # add this line
> podman start test
> echo $? >> /tmp/test.txt
> ```

Thanks, it works. How do you know that😺

> > [script] test_start.sh
> > ```
> > #! /bin/bash
> > export PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin # add this line
> > podman start test
> > echo $? >> /tmp/test.txt
> > ```
> 
> Thanks, it works. How do you know that😺

This is a very common problem with Linux, just like the first contact Linux is searching to withdraw from the interaction of the VI command.
/close

@zhangguanzhang: Closing this issue.

<details>

In response to [this](https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/12800#issuecomment-1013039296):

>> > [script] test_start.sh
>> > ```
>> > #! /bin/bash
>> > export PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin # add this line
>> > podman start test
>> > echo $? >> /tmp/test.txt
>> > ```
>> 
>> Thanks, it works. How do you know that😺
>
>This is a very common problem with Linux, just like the first contact Linux is searching to withdraw from the interaction of the VI command.
>/close


Instructions for interacting with me using PR comments are available [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/pull-requests.md).  If you have questions or suggestions related to my behavior, please file an issue against the [kubernetes/test-infra](https://github.com/kubernetes/test-infra/issues/new?title=Prow%20issue:) repository.
</details>
